step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{B} Wilson’s disease is characterized by excessive copper accumulation, not iron. It is treated with copper-chelating agents like trientine, not iron-based treatments. Therefore, it is incorrectly described as an iron storage disease.
raw_response: \boxed{B} Wilson’s disease is characterized by excessive copper accumulation, not iron. It is treated with copper-chelating agents like trientine, not iron-based treatments. Therefore, it is incorrectly described as an iron storage disease.
